basic_profile.legal_system: Civil law; federal state of 26 cantons. Income tax has federal + cantonal + communal layers; wealth tax and inheritance/gift tax are cantonal

capital_controls.value: No capital controls; stable, AAA-rated, mature private-banking sector

general_attitude_bitcoin.value: Bitcoin is a taxable asset (not legal tender); folded into existing law via the DLT Act; the cantonal dimension means there is no single 'Swiss' answer for wealth/inheritance tax

tax_residence_basis.value: Unlimited (worldwide) liability via domicile/centre of life, >=30 days' continuous presence with gainful activity, or >=90 days without

capital_gains_treatment.value: Gains on PRIVATE movable assets (incl. Bitcoin) are income-tax EXEMPT at all three levels (DBG art. 16(3)) — the core reason private Swiss crypto sales are effectively 0% income-taxed

wealth_tax.value: Privately held Bitcoin is included in taxable net wealth, valued at the FTA year-end rate; wealth tax is CANTONAL/communal (no federal wealth tax), progressive and generally well under ~1%, varying by canton

crypto_specific_guidance.value: The private-investor exemption is LOST if the taxpayer is deemed a professional/commercial securities trader (FTA Circular 36 safe-harbour criteria); then gains are ordinary income plus social contributions. Exact criteria are Tier-2 corroborated, definitionally stable

self_custody_allowed.value: Holding your own keys is not a regulated activity; private self-custody is permitted. Custody FOR third parties is regulated

direction.value: No standalone crypto statute; crypto folded into existing law via the DLT Act (in force Aug 2021), creating ledger-based securities and the FINMA DLT trading-facility license (first granted Mar 2025). New FINMA crypto-institution categories expected from ~2027

crs_participation.value: Switzerland participates in CRS (in force 2017; first exchange 2018; 100+ partners)

carf_status.value: Switzerland approved adopting the OECD CARF; the crypto information exchange was postponed to 2027 at the earliest

forced_heirship.value: Forced heirship (Pflichtteil/réserve) under the Civil Code; the 2023 reform (in force 1 Jan 2023) reduced descendants' reserved portion from 3/4 to 1/2 of the intestate share, abolished parents' compulsory portion, and left the spouse's 1/2 unchanged — enlarging the freely disposable quota

inheritance_tax.value: No federal inheritance or gift tax; levied by cantons. Spouses/registered partners exempt in ALL cantons; direct descendants exempt in MOST but not all (e.g. taxed in Appenzell Innerrhoden, Neuchâtel, Vaud per these sources). Confirm per canton

trust_recognition.value: Foreign trusts recognized via the Hague Trust Convention (in force in CH from 1 July 2007), but recognition can be overridden under art. 15 by mandatory Swiss law, notably forced heirship. No domestic Swiss trust exists yet

crypto_banking_friction.value: Deep private-banking market; crypto-banking exists but is selective (regulated crypto banks hold FINMA licenses; many traditional banks remain cautious). Ledger-based securities carry statutory insolvency segregation

investor_residency_programs.value: Lump-sum (expenditure-based) taxation is available to qualifying foreigners without Swiss gainful activity, but it is a CANTONAL option abolished in ~5 cantons (Zurich, Schaffhausen, Appenzell Ausserrhoden, Basel-Landschaft, Basel-Stadt)

tax_residence_triggers.value: Domicile, >=30 days with gainful activity, or >=90 days without, trigger unlimited tax liability

single_sig_self_custody.value: Private long-term holders benefit from the capital-gains exemption on movable assets; high-frequency trading risks professional-trader reclassification and loss of the exemption

inheritance.value: The 2023 reform enlarges the freely disposable quota (useful for directing Bitcoin to chosen heirs/structures), but forced heirship still constrains foreign trusts via Hague art. 15

direction_of_travel.label: self-custody-friendly
direction_of_travel.rationale: Private-investor capital-gains exemption and asset (not legal-tender) treatment are long-settled; low cantonal wealth tax
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Professional-trader reclassification removes the exemption; CARF + 2026 AML/UBO reform end reporting opacity

direction_of_travel.label: institution-friendly
direction_of_travel.rationale: DLT Act + new FINMA crypto-institution categories formalize custodians/VASPs; activity-based, innovation-tolerant vs EU MiCA
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Raises the bar for custodians; not loosening

direction_of_travel.label: reporting-heavy
direction_of_travel.rationale: CRS since 2017, CARF adoption (exchange ~2027), strict travel rule, 2026 AML/UBO register reform
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Banking secrecy for tax purposes is effectively gone for residents of CRS partners

open_questions.question: Exact FTA Circular 36 safe-harbour criteria and current professional-trader practice for high-frequency Bitcoin trading (cantonal application varies)
Per-canton inheritance/gift treatment of descendants and per-canton wealth-tax rates on crypto; ICTax year-end valuation method
2026 AML/UBO register scope and whether it touches self-custodians or trust structures
Interaction of Hague art. 15 forced-heirship override with Swiss-resident settlors holding Bitcoin in foreign trusts
Lump-sum minimum thresholds and which cantons retain the regime

watchlist.item: CARF crypto information exchange (~2027); CRS expansion
2026 AML reform / central UBO register; FATF 5th-round evaluation 2026-2027
New FINMA crypto-institution / payment-instrument categories expected ~2027

product_routing_notes: Switzerland users (HNW / family-office contrast) often surface jurisdictional and estate-structuring questions. The federal/cantonal split and professional-trader reclassification are the key counsel triggers.

change_log.change: Initial profile from parallel research pass. Strong Tier-1 (FTA/FINMA/SIF/admin.ch/EFD/OECD) coverage; cantonal items flagged as varying by canton; Circular 36 exact criteria flagged for primary confirmation.
